2606.17924.title: PearlVLA: Progressive Embodied Action-Plan Refinement in Latent Space
2606.17924.paper_summary: Current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models face a trade-off between efficient action generation and explicit deliberation. Directly decoding actions from vision-language backbone representations enables low-latency control, whereas explicit reasoning through textual chains, pixel-level subgoals, or action search can improve planning but incurs substantial latency and computational cost. We propose PearlVLA, a VLA framework that moves deliberation into the latent space of a vision-language model (VLM). PearlVLA separates VLM meta-query representations into a fixed visual grounding branch and an iterative latent plan branch. At each refinement round, a plan-conditioned world query probes a lightweight frozen latent world model for an action-free future observation latent, which is fed back to guide plan refinement. A future-guided RefineNet then applies scheduled residual updates to progressively refine a coarse semantic draft into a fine-grained latent action plan. The refined plan after K rounds is then decoded in parallel into an action chunk for low-latency execution. We further introduce Causal Refinement-Grouped Process-Reward RL to optimize the latent refinement process with rewards from longer-horizon imagined futures induced by latent plan edits. Empirical evaluations on the LIBERO benchmark demonstrate that PearlVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance among existing methods.
2606.17924.paper_summary_zh: 目前的視覺-語言-行動 (VLA) 模型面臨著高效行動生成與明確思考之間的權衡。從視覺-語言主幹表示直接解碼行動可以實現低延遲控制，而通過文本鏈、像素級子目標或行動搜索進行明確推理可以改善規劃，但會產生相當大的延遲和計算成本。我們提出了 PearlVLA，一個將思考移入視覺-語言模型 (VLM) 潛在空間的 VLA 框架。PearlVLA 將 VLM 元查詢表示分為固定的視覺基礎分支和迭代的潛在計劃分支。在每個精煉回合中，基於計劃的世界查詢探測一個輕量級的凍結潛在世界模型，以獲取一個無行動的未來觀察潛在，該潛在被反饋以指導計劃的精煉。然後，未來引導的 RefineNet 應用預定的殘差更新，逐步將粗略的語義草稿精煉為細粒度的潛在行動計劃。經過 K 輪的精煉後，該計劃將並行解碼為一個行動塊，以實現低延遲執行。我們進一步引入因果精煉分組過程獎勵強化學習，以利用潛在計劃編輯引導的長期想像未來的獎勵來優化潛在精煉過程。在 LIBERO 基準上的實證評估顯示，PearlVLA 在現有方法中達到了最先進的性能。
2606.17924.author: Bochen Yang et.al.
